title: centaurea pubescens subsp. pubescens
intro: Centaurea pubescens subsp. pubescens, also known as the hairy knapweed or giant knapweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been used to treat conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and relief from nausea and vomiting. Centaurea pubescens subsp. pubescens is often prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal and therapeutic uses.
